recapPrompt.prompt: Why is XOR so good at pair problems?

choices.label: Because equal values cancel each other out
choices.feedback: Correct. XOR turns matching pairs into zero.

choices.label: Because XOR sorts the numbers first
choices.feedback: Not this one. XOR is about canceling and combining bits, not sorting.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why checking the last bit and shifting right make a good team.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the bit clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for pair canceling, counting 1 bits, and building answers bit by bit.
hints: Number of 1 Bits checks the last bit and shifts.
Reverse Bits builds the answer one bit at a time.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main bit-manipulation clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck pair canceling, bit counting, and bit-by-bit building.

blanks.label: Main clue for building bit answers
expectedValues: build one bit at a time
grow the answer bit by bit

visibleTests.label: Build clue
visibleTests.expected: build one bit at a time
